title: EDEN experiment config
description: Declarative input to an EDEN experiment. Fields here are the host-mechanism-independent core: objective, evaluation schema, concurrency bound. Termination is selected via the termination_policy field (see 03-roles.md §6.2 decision-type 0); the pre-12a-3 top-level max_variants / max_wall_time / convergence_window / target_condition scalar fields have been removed from the normative schema — their semantics now round-trip as termination_policy.kind values (deployments that still carry the old top-level fields MAY do so as additional properties — conforming implementations ignore them). Worker-host per-task SLA deadlines (ideation_task_deadline / execution_task_deadline / evaluation_task_deadline) and the orchestrator quiescence budget (max_quiescent_iterations) are likewise implementation-defined config fields. Role bindings (how the ideator, executor, and evaluator are hosted — local subprocess, HTTP service, etc.) are implementation-defined and travel under additional properties; chapter 03 defines the role contracts but explicitly defers a normative role-binding representation to a future v0 addition or a v1 chapter (see 03-roles.md §6).

expr.description: Scalar expression over fields of evaluation_schema.

dispatch_mode.description: Per-decision-type gate on orchestrator-role automation; see 02-data-model.md §2.4 and 03-roles.md §6.1. The four operational keys default to 'auto'; the 'termination' key defaults to 'manual' (backward-compatible with pre-12a-3 deployments that had no termination policy). An omitted operational key is equivalent to 'auto'; an omitted 'termination' is equivalent to 'manual'. Unknown keys are tolerated and ignored by conforming implementations.

termination.description: When 'auto', the orchestrator consults the deployment-supplied termination policy each iteration (03-roles.md §6.2 decision-type 0). When 'manual' (the default), no termination policy is consulted; an operator MAY still terminate via POST /v0/experiments/{E}/terminate regardless of this flag.

ideation_creation.description: When 'auto', the orchestrator runs its ideation-creation policy. When 'manual', operators in the 'admins' group drive ideation-task creation via POST /tasks.

execution_dispatch.description: When 'auto', the orchestrator creates one execution task per ready idea. When 'manual', operators in the 'admins' or 'orchestrators' group drive execution-task creation via POST /tasks (the body MAY carry an explicit target override; otherwise idea.intended_executor is the seed).

evaluation_dispatch.description: When 'auto', the orchestrator creates one evaluation task per starting variant with commit_sha set. When 'manual', operators in the 'admins' group drive evaluation-task creation via POST /tasks.

integration.description: When 'auto', the orchestrator invokes the integrator on success variants. When 'manual', the operator drives integration via POST /variants/{T}/integrate.

ideation_policy.description: Declarative selection of the ideation-creation policy invoked by the orchestrator when dispatch_mode.ideation_creation == 'auto' (see 02-data-model.md §2.4 and 03-roles.md §6.2 decision-type 1). The policy mechanism is implementation-defined; this field selects one of the named policies a conforming reference impl ships. Two kinds are defined: 'maintain_pending' (refill the pending-ideation queue to a target depth, with an optional lifetime cap) and 'fixed_total' (create exactly N total ideation tasks across the experiment, then stop). Unknown keys are tolerated and ignored, consistent with dispatch_mode's posture (§2.4). When omitted, an implementation MAY pick its own default; the reference impl defaults to maintain_pending(target=3).

kind.description: Named policy kind. Conforming implementations MUST accept the two values defined here; unrecognized kinds MUST fail config validation rather than silently fall back.

target.description: Target pending-ideation queue depth (kind='maintain_pending' only). Default: 3.

max_total.description: Optional hard cap on lifetime ideation tasks (kind='maintain_pending' only). null disables the cap; an integer value clamps lifetime ideation creation to that many tasks.

total.description: Hard cap on lifetime ideation tasks (kind='fixed_total' only). REQUIRED for fixed_total; the orchestrator creates exactly this many ideation tasks across the experiment's lifetime, then stops.

termination_policy.description: Declarative selection of the termination policy invoked by the orchestrator when dispatch_mode.termination == 'auto' (see 02-data-model.md §2.4 and 03-roles.md §6.2 decision-type 0). Cousin of ideation_policy. The five named kinds are the reference policies; conforming implementations MUST accept the listed kinds. Unknown kinds MUST fail config validation rather than silently fall back. When dispatch_mode.termination == 'auto' the field is required (enforced by an experiment-config-level allOf-if-then over dispatch_mode.termination); when 'manual' (the default) the field is ignored. The mechanism is implementation-defined per 03-roles.md §6.

kind.description: Named policy kind. Conforming implementations MUST accept the five values defined here; unrecognized kinds MUST fail config validation rather than silently fall back.

target.description: kind='max_variants' only — hard ceiling on attempted variants. REQUIRED for max_variants.

duration.description: kind='max_wall_time' only — ISO 8601 duration string (e.g. 'PT2H'). MUST parse as a positive (> 0) timedelta; the FormatChecker rejects unparseable strings and zero/negative durations. The orchestrator's build_termination_policy() factory parses to timedelta at the boundary. REQUIRED for max_wall_time.

metric.description: kind in {'convergence_window', 'target_condition'} — evaluation key to read. REQUIRED for both kinds.

window.description: kind='convergence_window' only — trailing window of integrated variants. REQUIRED for convergence_window.

threshold.description: kind='target_condition' only — comparison threshold. REQUIRED for target_condition.

direction.description: kind in {'convergence_window', 'target_condition'} — direction of optimization. Defaults to 'maximize'.

baseline.description: Optional baseline-variant block (see 02-data-model.md §2.7 and §9.4). Controls whether the experiment seed is elevated to a first-class kind=='baseline' variant. Absent is equivalent to {enabled: true} (default-on, real evaluation). When 'metrics' is present the orchestrator stamps those metrics and creates the baseline directly in 'success', skipping evaluation dispatch; the metrics MUST validate against evaluation_schema (a runtime check, like variant.evaluation — §9.2). Supplying 'metrics' while enabled is false is a config error (suppressing a baseline while supplying its metrics).

enabled.description: When true (the default), the orchestrator auto-creates the seed baseline variant. When false, baseline creation is suppressed entirely and the seed stays out of the variant table.

metrics.description: Optional config-supplied evaluation metrics. When present, the orchestrator creates the baseline already terminal ('success' + these metrics) and skips evaluation dispatch. Keys MUST be a subset of evaluation_schema and values MUST match the declared types; like variant.evaluation this is a runtime check (§9.2), not expressible in this schema.

auto_checkpoint.description: Implementation-defined deployment behavior (NOT a protocol contract): when enabled, the reference orchestrator periodically exports a portable checkpoint (07-wire-protocol.md §14.1) and, optionally, one on experiment termination. Opt-in; absent is equivalent to {enabled: false}. The destination directory is deployment wiring (orchestrator --auto-checkpoint-dir / $EDEN_AUTO_CHECKPOINT_DIR), not part of this portable block, because a host filesystem path means nothing on a different deployment.

enabled.description: Master switch. Default false (absent block is equivalent to {enabled: false}).

interval_seconds.description: Cadence in seconds between periodic checkpoints (matches the *_task_deadline seconds convention). Default 3600 (60 minutes). The first periodic checkpoint fires one full interval after orchestrator startup.

retention_count.description: Ring-buffer depth for periodic checkpoints; the oldest periodic archives beyond this count are pruned. Default 6. Does not bound terminal checkpoints (those are kept outside the ring).

on_terminate.description: Take a terminal checkpoint when the experiment reaches state=terminated. Default true.

max_quiescent_iterations.description: Orchestrator quiescence budget — a polling orchestrator exits after N consecutive no-progress iterations (the orchestrator role is single-shot-or-polling per 03-roles.md §6, so this count is non-normative). MUST be >= 2; N=1 risks exiting while a worker is mid-submit. When omitted, the reference orchestrator uses 3 (manual-UI sessions want a much higher value — see operator playbook). Implementation-defined. Read only in single-experiment-mode orchestrator startup; multi-experiment-mode keeps the existing --max-quiescent-iterations CLI flag pending #214.

ideation_task_deadline.description: Seconds the ideator host waits for a single ideation_command invocation to produce its idea-list payload. Default: 120.0. Implementation-defined; bounds the per-task worker-host SLA.

execution_task_deadline.description: Seconds the executor host waits for a single execution_command invocation to write its variant. Default: 600.0. Implementation-defined; bounds the per-task worker-host SLA.

evaluation_task_deadline.description: Seconds the evaluator host waits for a single evaluation_command invocation to produce its metrics. Default: 300.0. Implementation-defined; bounds the per-task worker-host SLA.
